title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: July 03, 2026: US Markets Closed for Holiday, Reflecting Thursday's Mixed Close

items.headline: US Markets Closed for Independence Day Holiday; Thursday Saw Mixed Close
items.summary: US equity markets are closed today, July 3rd, observing the Independence Day holiday, with trading activity reflecting Thursday's mixed session. The Dow Jones Industrial Average surged to a new record high, while the S&P 500 remained largely flat. The Nasdaq Composite, however, experienced a decline, primarily due to continued weakness in the semiconductor sector. Sentiment was influenced by softer-than-expected US jobs data, which helped to temper concerns about imminent interest rate hikes.

items.headline: Apple Rises on iPhone Launch Reports
items.summary: Apple shares climbed on reports hinting at upcoming iPhone launches.

items.headline: ClearOne Soars on Merger Agreement
items.summary: ClearOne saw a significant jump after announcing a merger agreement with Cortigent, Inc.

items.headline: CaliberCos Gains on Tokenization Strategy
items.summary: CaliberCos surged as it advanced its real estate fund tokenization strategy.

items.headline: Tesla Falls Despite Strong Deliveries
items.summary: Tesla shares declined despite the company reporting stronger-than-expected delivery estimates for the quarter.

items.headline: Meta Drops on AI Capacity Concerns
items.summary: Meta Platforms saw a loss amidst renewed concerns regarding AI capacity.

items.headline: Micron Leads Chip Sector Decline
items.summary: Micron was among several chipmakers that fell for a second day as investors questioned AI optimism and valuations.

items.headline: June Jobs Report Disappoints, Easing Rate Hike Fears
items.summary: The US economy added a much lower-than-expected 57,000 jobs in June, significantly below forecasts. While the unemployment rate ticked down to 4.2%, this was primarily attributed to a substantial decrease in the labor force, pushing participation down to 61.5%. This softer jobs data has led markets to temper expectations for immediate Federal Reserve rate hikes.

items.headline: Chip Sector Continues to Lag Amid AI Valuation Concerns
items.summary: The technology sector, particularly semiconductor stocks, was a significant drag on the market, extending a two-day slump. The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index was notably down 5.4%, as investors continued to question the high valuations stemming from AI optimism. This contrasts with improved breadth in other sectors.

items.headline: Fed Holds Rates Steady; Debate Intensifies on Future Hikes
items.summary: The Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) maintained its benchmark federal funds target range at 3.50-3.75 percent at its June meeting. Despite the recent weak jobs report easing immediate pressure, debate is intensifying over the possibility of a rate hike before year-end, driven by elevated inflation and soaring energy prices.

items.headline: Google Loses Final Appeal on EU Android Antitrust Fine
items.summary: Google lost its final appeal against a substantial €4.1 billion ($4.7 billion) European Union antitrust fine. The EU's highest court upheld the penalty, which was imposed due to Google's abuse of its dominant market position through Android-related licensing and pre-installation practices.

items.headline: Gold Rises as Dollar Weakens, Oil Sees Modest Gains
items.summary: Gold prices gained for a third consecutive day, approaching $4,200, supported by a weaker US dollar and reduced expectations for aggressive rate hikes following the soft jobs report. Brent crude oil and US benchmark crude also registered modest increases.

items.headline: Q2 Earnings Season Approaches, No Major Reports Today
items.summary: The second-quarter earnings season is set to begin in earnest during the second full week of July. No noteworthy earnings announcements are scheduled for Friday, July 3rd. Companies like STMicroelectronics ($STM) are scheduled to release Q2 earnings on July 23rd.
